mappings.source: A1 — visual quality is not mechanically checkable
mappings.note: Mechanical Verification Model.md lists prose/UX/strategy/ethics as UNVERIFIABLE:OUT_OF_SCOPE; visual design belongs in this category.

mappings.source: A2 — named human reviewer required for UNVERIFIABLE
mappings.note: Compensation contract: human_review block with by, process, trigger.

mappings.source: A3 — v1 panels must remain available
mappings.note: v1 10-panel narrative + v1 panel SVGs are git-tracked; both Comic.md and Comic_v1.md are in mkdocs nav.

mappings.source: A4 — structural validator checks form, not expression
mappings.note: Validator asserts dimensions, palette hex, Maya data-attribute. It does not check emotional transmission. The verifier confirms a green build of something that may not communicate its purpose.

mappings.source: A5 — refinement must describe intentions, not primitives
mappings.note: v3 refinement must include an Aesthetic Invariants section: emotional beat, character focus, background role, continuity. These describe what each panel must communicate, not just what it must contain.

mappings.source: A6 — Known Limitations L4 is the canonical reference
mappings.note: L4 names refinement as minimal-by-design and not validated. This packet is a worked example of how L4 manifests.

mappings.source: A7 — v3 packet with Aesthetic Invariants
mappings.note: v3 will introduce Aesthetic Invariants as a refinement section and require named-reviewer sign-off for any visual artifact.
The current meta-state of the methodology is 'detected': the regression has been named, but v3 is not yet implemented.
When v3 lands, the meta-state advances to v3_planned and Aesthetic Invariants become the required refinement section for visual packets.
v1 panels cannot be silently deleted; deletion requires a visible git diff.
